name: Cumbria (United Kingdom)
description: A field divided per fess green over navy, with a wide white wavy stripe near the lower edge and a yellow rose with green leaves at the centre.
history: Designed by Iain Johnson in 2008; registered with the Flag Institute as the flag of Cumbria. Symbolises the lakes (white wavy band), the fells (green), the sea (blue) and the parnassus or 'Grass of Parnassus' flower.

features.symbolism: green for the fells, blue for the sea
